title: Cozy and Briny Dutch Lamb Braised
description: A hearty, comforting lamb dish featuring lamb loin chops, boiled potatoes, and a sweet and savory Dutch stew inspired flavor profile.

ingredients.name: beets
ingredients.notes: +/- as desired for added color & flavor

instructions: Season the lamb loin chops with salt and black pepper.
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb loin chops until browned on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter to the pot and sauté the onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the boiled potatoes, beets, white wine, vegetable broth, tomato paste, bay leaves, black peppercorns, and thyme to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the flour to the pot and stir to combine.
Return the lamb loin chops to the pot and simmer for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the lamb reaches a desired level of doneness.
Serve the lamb with the braised potatoes and vegetables.
Garnish with fresh thyme, if desired.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Skim off any excess fat from the surface of the sauce.
Serve the sauce over the lamb and potatoes.
Discard the bay leaves and black peppercorns before serving.
